code_change_summary.autograd_refcount: Shared-forward native compact handles now get one retain per autograd node that will call the inverse collective, and one release after that inverse collective runs.
code_change_summary.default_release_policy: Released handles are removed from the cache by default. TORCHTITAN_STANDARD_EP_MORI_NATIVE_COMPACT_KEEP_RELEASED_HANDLES=1 preserves the old persistent-handle behavior for perf A/Bs.
code_change_summary.gc_policy: Release does not force Python gc.collect() unless TORCHTITAN_STANDARD_EP_MORI_NATIVE_COMPACT_GC_ON_RELEASE=1 is set.
code_change_summary.perf_intent: Dictionary retain/release at autograd node boundaries only; no compact-row path work, no route-mask adapter, no row materialization, and no per-token refcounting.

result.failure: HSA_STATUS_ERROR_OUT_OF_RESOURCES in the next backward after step 1

comparison.read: Reference-counted release lowers logged peak slightly but does not move the failure boundary; remaining memory pressure is not just stale shared forward handles.

read: The reference-counted handle lifetime patch is correct and low-overhead, but it is not sufficient to make fixed-cap native compact MBS8 stable. The next source target should reduce active compact capacity/staging output residency or collapse the MORI staging/output ABI, rather than keep optimizing global handle-cache lifetime.
